subject: Project Management
title: Testing Phase
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Testing Phase.

hook.text: In waterfall, testing happens after all coding is complete. Bugs discovered here are the most expensive to fix.

buildup.text: Testers compare the finished product against the requirements specification, checking every documented feature.

discovery.text: IBM's Systems Sciences Institute found that fixing a bug in testing costs 15x more than fixing it in requirements.

twist.text: Late testing means defects pile up for months undetected. The 'testing phase' often doubles in length as bugs surface.

climax.text: Modern approaches test continuously from day one. Waterfall's late testing phase is its most expensive mistake.

punchline.text: Finding bugs at the end costs the most. Test early or pay late.

quiz.question: Why is testing late in waterfall problematic?
options: Bugs found late are far more expensive to fix than those caught early
Late testing always finds all bugs
Testing early is more expensive than testing late
